channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: He filmed himself doing household tasks — for AI robots
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Reece Rogers of WIRED about a new wave of data collection marketplaces, where users can sell their videos of everyday tasks to AI developers.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Reece Rogers of WIRED about a new wave of data collection marketplaces, where users can sell their videos of everyday tasks to AI developers.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: An art museum in Maine tries to balance protecting art and saving energy
item.description: Keeping a museum's temperature and humidity constant -- rain or shine, all year long -- takes a massive amount of energy, and it's expensive. But some museums have a solution.
item.content:encoded: Keeping a museum's temperature and humidity constant -- rain or shine, all year long -- takes a massive amount of energy, and it's expensive. But some museums have a solution.
item.dc:creator: Molly Enking

item.title: A National Spelling Bee champion shares what it takes to win
item.description: The 2025 Scripps National Spelling Bee champ shares what it takes to spell it right in 2026.
item.content:encoded: The 2025 Scripps National Spelling Bee champ shares what it takes to spell it right in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Visual impairments don't keep these birders out of the hobby
item.description: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.content:encoded: The phrase "bird watching" does not take in the full range of people who love searching for wild birds. We meet a few of the many visually impaired birders who use their ears.
item.dc:creator: Nancy Eve Cohen

item.title: Checking in with swing state voters on how gas prices are affecting them
item.description: The price of a gallon of gas is up well over a dollar from where it was a year ago and these swing voters are feeling it.
item.content:encoded: The price of a gallon of gas is up well over a dollar from where it was a year ago and these swing voters are feeling it.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: A wildfire off the California coast threatened one of the world's rarest pines
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Heather Schneider of the Santa Barbara Botanic Garden about the garden's efforts to conserve seeds of rare plants from Santa Rosa Island, where a wildfire just burned.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Heather Schneider of the Santa Barbara Botanic Garden about the garden's efforts to conserve seeds of rare plants from Santa Rosa Island, where a wildfire just burned.
item.dc:creator: Scott Detrow

item.title: A high-stakes U.S. Senate Race in Texas is expected to be a bitter contest
item.description: A U.S. Senate race in Texas is now set with the state's Attorney General Ken Paxton winning the GOP nomination in a primary runoff Tuesday. He'll face Democratic Senate candidate James Talarico.
item.content:encoded: A U.S. Senate race in Texas is now set with the state's Attorney General Ken Paxton winning the GOP nomination in a primary runoff Tuesday. He'll face Democratic Senate candidate James Talarico.
item.dc:creator: Blaise Gainey

item.title: Immigration detention center known as 'Alligator Alcatraz' may soon close
item.description: A detention center in the Florida Everglades dubbed "Alligator Alcatraz" has become too expensive to maintain and may soon close.
item.content:encoded: A detention center in the Florida Everglades dubbed "Alligator Alcatraz" has become too expensive to maintain and may soon close.
item.dc:creator: Meghan Bowman

item.title: Remembering 'It Takes Two' rapper Rob Base
item.description: It Takes Two rapper Rob Base died at 59 after a battle with cancer. His music, made with his childhood friend DJ E-Z Rock, filled dancefloors.
item.content:encoded: It Takes Two rapper Rob Base died at 59 after a battle with cancer. His music, made with his childhood friend DJ E-Z Rock, filled dancefloors.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Measles outbreak rages in Bangladesh
item.description: A measles outbreak in Bangladesh is suspected to have killed more than 500 and sickened up to 60,000. Bangladesh was getting measles under control until a new government upended vaccination efforts.
item.content:encoded: A measles outbreak in Bangladesh is suspected to have killed more than 500 and sickened up to 60,000. Bangladesh was getting measles under control until a new government upended vaccination efforts.
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel
